text.title: Database Design Worked Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked example for the database-design methodology.

example.title: Orders + line-items schema
example.narrative: Service tracks orders with multiple line items. Entities: customers, products, orders, order_items, addresses, payments. 6 tables, FKs on every relationship, UNIQUE(order_id, line_no) on order_items, CHECK(amount_cents >= 0). Indexes on (customer_id, created_at) for the dashboard list query and (status, created_at) for the worker queue scan — confirmed by EXPLAIN. Migration plan: expand-then-contract because we're splitting a legacy `orders.shipping_address_text` JSONB column into a normalized `addresses` table. Step 1: add `addresses` + `orders.shipping_address_id` nullable. Step 2: backfill + dual-write. Step 3 (later release): drop `shipping_address_text`.
example.artifact: schema-spec.md + erd.png + migration-plan.md, all reproducible from CREATE TABLE DDL.
